paragraphs: No more costly disruptions in your power system because CareLabs’ relay coordination study brings precision for maximum protection. Our study solutions keep your system running.

Protective relays in an electrical system are meant to trip in a sequence when a fault occurs. CareLabs’ Relay Coordination Study in Canada reflects careful timing, coordination, and accuracy required for safety and compliance needs.
A detailed analysis to ensure that the protective relays work in the correct sequence to prevent outages and damage to the system.
Relay Coordination Study is critical for extensive electrical systems. These studies ensure system reliability, prevent cascading faults, and reduce maintenance costs.
Let us know your needs. We offer expert electrical inspections for all industries in Canada.

CareLabs is a frontrunner in offering relay coordination study services in Canada. By integrating best practices, and advanced tools, our relay coordination study reports are highly-recognized by multifarious industries.

A medium-sized manufacturing facility was experiencing frequent relay malfunctions and shutdowns. The facility management sought the help of the CareLabs Relay Coordination Study to reduce the downtime.

We collected the data needed to simulate the system, and using advanced tools, we conducted a coordination analysis. Our recommendations involved optimized relay settings and coordination. By implementing our recommendations, the company could reduce unnecessary trips by 60%. With enhanced equipment protection and operational efficiency, we got a satisfied client. CareLabs’ proactive solutions fostered a culture of safety and compliance within the company.

To get the best safety results, carefully selecting and coordinating the relay setting is pivotal. CareLabs’ phased approach in relay coordination study services enables industries across Canada to achieve maximum assessment results.
From data analysis to coordination strategy, this phase covers it all.
Simulation, implementation, and review are the main steps followed during this phase.

With the changing needs of the industries, we at CareLabs offer dynamic solutions to our clients. We helped shape the electrical safety journey of many multinational companies around the world. Our comprehensive relay coordination study reports and compliance standards bring our clients back to us for their electrical safety needs.
Find answers to common questions about our electrical installation and maintenance services.

Protective relays must be designed to trip in the correct sequence under fault conditions in an electrical system. A Relay Coordination Study analyzes the power system to ensure that these relays are working in coordination.
To bring uniformity, Canada has a set of standards for relay coordination. CSA C22.1 or the Canadian Electrical Code is the foundational standard for compliance in relay coordination study.
A relay Coordination study is conducted after the design phase of the electrical system, and when major changes are made in the system.
At CareLabs, we use ETAP 22.5 for relay coordination studies in Canada.
Reach out to us by giving us a call or signing up. Our executive will contact you and guide you through the strategy.
Experience value-laden electrical safety inspections and arc flash study services tailored to your needs across multiple regions and industries in Canada.
bulletLists: Compliance with standards- We have certifications showcasing compliance to standards like NFPA 70E, IEEE, CSA Z462, etc.
Customized solutions- We at CareLabs understand the needs of our clients and offer tailored relay coordination study services.
Credentials- We have experience in completing many protective relay coordination study services, that exceed our client’s expectations.
Cost and Value- Our solutions are cost-effective and add more value to the safety of your electrical system.
Step 1- Gather data like relay details, and its settings.
Step 2- Create an SLD model using specialized software like ETAP 22.5 to determine the protective relay positions
Step 3- Conduct a relay coordination simulation to find out the right fault-clearing sequence.
Step 4- Recommend the final relay settings, periodically review and update the relay settings.
Tailored solutions based on the specific needs and operating conditions of your facility. This helps ensure safety and efficiency.
We use advanced tools and techniques like ETAP for precise relay coordination studies. This data-driven approach ensures reliable and accurate results.

With tools, we have the experience and expertise in power system study and Analysis. Our studies comply with the safety standards, both international such as NFPA 70E, and OSHA, and Canadian like CSA C22.1.
